title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh will run her third Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation in 2026.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh overcame chemo and surgeries to return to running.
McHugh runs to honor late friends and to support charitable causes, including the Joe Andruzzi Foundation.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others urge Meta to address privacy risks of facial recognition in smart glasses.
The senators warned the technology could identify protesters, chilling free expression and enabling harassment.
Meta has until April 6 to detail data handling and opt-out options for facial recognition in glasses.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million bond to purchase PFAS-free firefighting gear.
Firefighters urge immediate action as testing revealed PFAS in current gear, linked to cancer and health risks.
Some councilors seek more time to review the proposal due to cost concerns and gear durability questions.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes converting Capron Park Zoo to a nature reserve and rehabilitation center to tackle $57 million in repair costs.
The zoo will operate normally until September 7, then offer limited weekend access, before transitioning to native wildlife focus.
Zoo veterinarian criticizes the proposal, fearing it signals an eventual zoo closure; a transition team will form in July.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown organized a microwedding at Somerville's Bow Market after a blizzard disrupted their January 2026 plans.
Meredith Goldstein's column explores changing perceptions of relationship terms like 'partner' and their significance in modern dating.
Kara Baskin reflects on '90s communication nostalgia in her review of FX's 'Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette.'

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, of Burlington, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback lost his father to glioblastoma and survived testicular cancer, shaping his running journey.
After surviving a blood infection, Averback aims to honor his father and acknowledge survival through running.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard is working out with former Titans teammate and trade target A.J. Brown.
The Eagles have rejected an offer from the Patriots for A.J. Brown but may trade him post-June 1 for cap reasons.
Recent NFL trades suggest the Eagles will demand a high price, likely a first-round pick, for A.J. Brown.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completes his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney plans to discuss possible NHL debut with prospect James Hagens this spring.
Hagens, the Bruins' seventh pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, is expected to boost Boston's lineup soon.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett and directed by Bradley Cooper is streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man', set during WWII, is available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl', a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is streaming on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora remains non-committal about his future as Red Sox manager beyond his current contract, which ends in 2027.
Cora, earning over $7 million per season, is focused on the present and enjoys the team's current state.
He needs 134 more wins to surpass Terry Francona for second-most managerial wins in Red Sox history.
